entries.summary: P1 turn 1: Trashed Copper, played Zap (1 damage, cycled), bought Gold. Board: raider-1 captured center-northeast (8,1); scout positioned at (9,4) toward center-center-south/east; raider-3 at (11,4) toward center-east; marksman at (9,2) toward center-center-north. Income 2 supply, 0 saved for recruits.
entries.reasoning: Rush strategy: contest centers early. Raider-1 (mov 4) moved 2 hexes west to capture center-northeast (8,1) — the only center reachable turn 1. Scout (mov 5) moved 5 hexes through friendly-occupied (11,1) to (9,4), positioning within 2 hexes of both center-center-south (7,5) and center-east (8,6) for next-turn capture. Raider-3 (mov 4) moved south to (11,4), within 3 hexes of center-east (8,6) for next-turn capture. Marksman (mov 2) moved to (9,2) heading toward center-center-north (5,3). No enemies in range; Zap damage unused. Deck: trashed Copper for thinning, bought Gold for economy to fund future damage purchases. No pending win threats (4v4 units, 1 center controlled).

entries.summary: P2 turn 2: Played Village+Smithy for 4 card draw, bought 2nd Village for engine. Board: scout captured center-center (6,5), raider captured center-west-south (3,7), guardian and marksman pushed east toward eastern centers. No combat possible. P2 now controls 2 centers vs P1's 1.
entries.reasoning: Deck: Engine strategy - Village first for +2 actions/+1 card, then Smithy for +3 cards, maximizing hand size. Bought Village to build action/draw engine. No trash needed yet; coppers provide early buying power. Board: Contested 2 supply centers aggressively. Scout used full movement 5 to reach center-center (6,5), the most central and valuable hex. Raider used 3 of 4 movement to capture center-west-south (3,7). Guardian moved to (2,8) and marksman to (2,9), both pushing east toward center-east (8,6) and center-southeast (8,7) for future turns. No enemies in range for attacks. Income next turn: 2+2=4 supply. No pending win threats.
